item.title: Unix Philosophy
item.description: Unix Philosophy I've been exploring the history of Unix, and one concept that keeps coming up is the Unix philosophy. But what exactly is it? To start, here's a quote from "The UNIX Programming Environment": Even though the UNIX system introduces a number of innovative programs and techniques, no single program or idea makes it work well. Instead, what makes it effective is the approach to programming, a philosophy of using the computer.

item.title: Library
item.description: This is a collection of books I’ve read (or at least started reading). Buddhism 選択本願念仏集 - 法然上人 仏教聖典 - 仏教伝道協会 観無量寿経 Collected Refutations of Heterodoxy - 蕅益智旭 Deus Destroyed - 不干斎ハビアン Heart Sutra with Commentary - 宣化 Honen The Buddhist Saint: Essential Writings and Official Biography - Joseph A. Fitzgerald (Editor) Laughing at Christianity - Kiyū Dōjin Lives of Great Monks and Nuns - 仏教伝道協会 佛說無量壽經 Mahayana Buddhism: The Doctrinal Foundations - Paul Williams मूलमध्यमककारिका - नागार्जुन 佛說阿彌陀經 和語灯録 - 法然上人 Confucianism 大學 - 曾子 中庸 - 子思 Deism The Age of Reason; Being an Investigation of True and Fabulous Theology - Thomas Paine Economy Economic Policy: Thoughts for Today and Tomorrow - Ludwig Heinrich Edler von Mises Exploitation Theory of Socialism-Communism: The Idea That All Unearned Income (Rent, Interest and Profit Involves Economic Injustice) - Eugen Böhm Ritter von Bawerk Metaphysics Παρμενίδης - Πλάτων Poetry Divine Comedy - Dante Alighieri Pop culture The Moe Manifesto: An Insider's Look at the Worlds of Manga, Anime, and Gaming - Patrick W.
